hero.headline: Making complex products easier to understand.
hero.description: I'm a product designer with experience across SaaS, frontend development, and teaching. I focus on clearer journeys, lower friction, and interfaces people can trust quickly.
hero.framing: I enjoy working on products where complexity has started to get in the way of users. My role is usually to untangle the workflow and make the interface feel obvious again.

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description: User interviews, analytics, and stakeholder conversations to identify where users are getting stuck.

steps.title: Simplify the system
steps.description: Mapping journeys, clarifying hierarchy, and reducing unnecessary complexity.

steps.title: Design with implementation in mind
steps.description: Working closely with engineers so improvements ship as real product changes, not just mockups.

whatIEnjoy.title: What I Enjoy Working On
items: Products where complexity has started to get in the way of users.
Interfaces that need stronger hierarchy and clearer information flow.
Platforms where analytics and user research can guide meaningful improvements.

workIntro: Simplifying journeys, tightening workflows, and improving how products feel to use.
writingIntro: I occasionally write about product design, UX, accessibility, and the thinking behind clearer digital products.

linkedin.title: For the professional side of the conversation, find me on LinkedIn.
linkedin.description: I share product thinking, writing, and the occasional reflection on design, accessibility, and teaching.

contact.title: If you're hiring, building something interesting, or just want to talk product design, feel free to get in touch.
contact.description: I'm open to product design roles, freelance work, and thoughtful collaborations. Send a few details and I'll come back to you.
